reasoning:
The most recent known value for the 30-day average of fatalities over the last 360 days is 0.000, as Oman has had no ACLED-recorded fatalities since late 2024 (a rare July 2024 ISIS attack falls outside the 360-day window). Therefore, the question essentially asks whether at least 1 fatality will occur in the 30 days prior to each resolution date. Oman is extremely stable and peaceful; fatal political violence or conflict is historically near zero (approx 1-2% probability for any given month).

For the November 30 resolution, 23 days of the 30-day window (Oct 31–Nov 30) have already passed with no reported events, leaving only a 7-day vulnerability period. This makes a positive resolution highly improbable. For the subsequent resolution dates (Dec 23 and Feb 21), the full 30-day windows are in the future, increasing the baseline chance slightly but still remaining deeply improbable. Because instructions mandate that probabilities be at least 0.05 and that uncertainty should visibly increase for distant dates, I set the estimates at the lower bound of 0.05 for Nov 30, and gradually increase to 0.06 and 0.08 for the later windows to respect the forecast horizon rule.
